intro:
There is no information available about the plant "Diasperus discolor." It's possible that this is a misspelling or incorrect name for a plant. However, based on a similar name "Disperus discolor" which appears to be an error, it's actually more likely that you are referring to "Dioscorea discolor". 

Dioscorea discolor is a type of plant commonly known as a yam, belonging to the Dioscoreaceae family. The health benefits of Dioscorea discolor include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dioscorea discolor include diosgenin, a plant steroid with reported anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as other compounds such as saponins and flavonoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Dioscorea discolor include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat a variety of ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
